title: Charges & payouts model (per development-assets/charges-payouts-data-model.md)
description: Implement the agreed articulation: every code usage logs a Charge (client-owed amount, possibly 0) tied to the code's order_type (training|complimentary|lead|sale — replaces the old type tag; values today resolve to 0 except sale, but the mechanism never gates on type). Payouts (category royalty|fee|residual, payout_type e.g. pro_d_royalty/tech_fee/language_fee/residual_margin) split each charge among stakeholders and must sum to the charge — residual_margin is the balance catch-all, always paid to a real stakeholder. Lead->sale conversion is NEVER stored; it is inferred at reporting time from an external_order_id carrying both a lead charge and a later sale charge (one lead, at most one sale, per external_order_id; ties only via external_order_id, never cross-key). Repeat usage of the same order+order_type logs a $0 charge referencing the original — this is what makes royalty due exactly once per order, replacing the flat_on_conversion term kind and any dedup-flag scheme. Payouts carry status accrued|paid|void for future aging reports.
acceptance_criteria: charges + payouts tables ledger every usage; order_type replaces type; payout schedule with residual balances to the charge; repeat-usage $0 charges reference the original; statement reports by order type/recipient/code + conversion inference; panel UI for charge + payout schedule config; docs/07 rewritten in the new vocabulary; all tests pass.
close_reason: Shipped and E2E-verified: charges + payouts ledgers, order types as reporting dimensions, per-code charge + payout schedule with residual catch-all, once-per-order structural dedup via $0 repeat charges, conversion inference in the statement, full panel rework. 49 tests pass incl. golden gate. Docs 07 rewritten; canonical doc committed.

title: Normalize clients in the DB + Stripe-based monthly usage-metered royalty billing
description:
User: 'clients and their details should be normalized in the db rather than free text. I think that we will use stripe to collect the royalties from the client by bank transfer on a monthly usage meter — the client would manage their billing in their account.' Research/scoping epic only — nothing implemented. See child issues for the two distinct asks.

## Current state (free-text client references found in codebase)
- No `clients` table/model exists anywhere in api/app/Models or the migrations.
- `api_keys.name` (api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php) is a free-text string, but the panel UI labels it 'Client name' (panel/src/views/Keys.tsx line 71: `<Field label="Client name">`) — i.e. today a 'client' is really just the human-readable name on an API key row, not a first-class entity. `ApiKey` (api/app/Models/ApiKey.php) has no `client_id` or relation to any client entity.
- `access_codes.issued_to` (api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php, nullable string) is a second, separate free-text 'who is this for' field, unrelated to `api_keys.name` — no FK, no guarantee the two match/refer to the same entity even when they should.
- `royalty_terms.recipient` (same migration) is a THIRD free-text string identifying who is owed a royalty — again no normalization/FK back to a client entity.
- platform/docs/07-access-codes-royalties.md 'Billing (designed now, switched on later)' section already anticipates Stripe: 'When Stripe is enabled: map billable clients → Stripe customers, roll up non-derivative fees_due line items into metered usage / invoices (Laravel Cashier)... Royalty statements (per client, per code type, per recipient, per period) must be producible from usage_events alone — no Stripe dependency for reporting.' So Stripe/Cashier was already the intended direction, but no `clients` table or Stripe customer mapping exists yet to hang it off of.
- No Cashier/Stripe package is installed (checked api/composer.json — not verified line-by-line here, follow up during implementation).
- No client self-service billing portal / account area exists (the control panel, panel/, is only the internal admin/ops panel per docs/08 — a client-facing account portal would be new surface area).

See child issues for the specific asks.
notes: STALE-CONTEXT NOTE (2026-07-13): opw.1 is DONE — clients + payees tables exist with FKs from api_keys/access_codes/payout_terms and panel CRUD. Vocabulary shifted since this epic was written: royalty_terms is now payout_terms, and metering/reporting runs off the charges + payouts ledgers (see docs/07 + development-assets/charges-payouts-data-model.md), not usage_events.fees_due. Remaining child: opw.2 (Stripe).

title: Rework access-code royalty model: revenue-split terms, named codes, language royalties, pay-on-conversion, retake tracking
description:
User (product owner) feedback while reviewing 127.0.0.1:8000/panel/codes: the current royalty model (code type = training|bizdev|derivative, each implying a royalty *policy*) is the wrong shape. In practice ALL issued codes have royalty terms — 'training'/'bizdev'/'derivative' are really just descriptions of a set of royalty agreements, and in the current business none of them actually owe royalties yet. He wants to move away from type-as-royalty-policy and toward: specify the total royalty payment expected (revenue) when a scoring is performed, and how it's divided among parties — which is actually already closer to what `royalty_terms` (plural, per-recipient) does today, so this is more a UX/reporting/labeling shift than a data-model rewrite, but there are several genuinely new requirements bundled in. This epic tracks the full conversation; see child issues for each distinct piece. Do not implement — research/scoping only per user's request.

## Current state (for reference)
- api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php: `access_codes.type` enum(training|bizdev|derivative) drives royalty *expectation* per platform/docs/07 table ('derivative' = no royalty due by convention), but the actual amounts live in `royalty_terms` (access_code_id, recipient, kind: flat_per_report|percentage_of_price|tiered|subscription, amount, currency, active, effective_from/until) — many-to-one per code already.
- `access_codes` has no `name` field — only `code` (opaque, e.g. `ac_xxxx`), `product_code`, `issued_to`. Royalty statements (CodesController::statement) group by recipient only, not by product/code name.
- No `language` column on `royalty_terms` or `usage_events` fees_due rows — royalty-per-language (e.g. paying a translator) isn't representable even though `assessments.language` and `scored_results.language` already exist.
- No royalty-kind for 'due only if converted' — the closest current concept is the `derivative` type (no royalty due), but there's no mechanism to later charge a royalty retroactively/on a follow-up event tied to the same person/order.
- Assessments (api/app/Models/Assessment.php, migration 2026_07_11_000002_create_assessment_tables.php) already have `external_id` (nullable, indexed with api_key_id) and support incremental tool submission + multiple `scored_results` rows per assessment (one per scoring call) — but there is no explicit 'order' entity distinct from 'assessment', and no cross-assessment linkage for the same respondent taking the test again (retakes create a wholly new `assessments` row with no back-reference).

See child issues for the specific asks.

title: Payout schedule: enforce currency consistency with the code's charge
description: AccessCode::computePayouts computes the residual as charge_amount − sum(other lines) treating all amounts as plain numbers — but payout_terms carry their own currency, so a USD 100 charge with a EUR 5 flat line yields residual 95 mixing currencies, silently wrong money. Panel add-line form also defaults currency USD independent of the code's charge currency. Fix options (pick one): (a) simplest — require every payout line's currency to equal the code's charge_currency (validate in CodesController addTerm/updateTerm + panel form locks the field), or (b) real multi-currency support with conversion, which nothing needs yet. Recommend (a). Files: api/app/Models/AccessCode.php computePayouts/line, api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/CodesController.php, panel/src/views/codes/CodeDetail.tsx.

title: Panel: Codes section redesign — dedicated pages, full CRUD-safe lifecycle
description: Redesign /panel/codes from a single dumped-together page into a proper multi-page flow: a list/browse page, a dedicated 'issue new code' page, and a per-code detail/manage page (metadata edit, royalty terms as a first-class sub-resource with add/edit/end, usage summary, revoke/restore). 'Full CRUD' here means Create/Read/Update/Deactivate — never hard-delete — because codes and royalty terms are referenced by historical usage_events and scored_results; deleting either would corrupt the audit trail and royalty-statement reproducibility docs/07 already guarantees ('add/end without deleting history'). Concretely: allowed_scopes and type become locked once a code has uses_count > 0 (changing them would retroactively misrepresent what earlier scoring calls were permitted to do); royalty terms become locked (no amount/recipient edits) once they've appeared in any usage_event's fees_due — editing a charged term would rewrite history for already-invoiced amounts, so the only path forward for a charged term is end + add a new one (already-supported pattern). Uncharged/unused terms and never-scored codes may be freely edited.
acceptance_criteria: List page, issue page, and detail page exist as separate routes under /panel/codes; detail page supports full metadata edit (locked fields explained in UI when uses_count>0); royalty terms support add/edit(when unused)/end; revoke/restore present; no delete endpoints introduced anywhere in this flow; browser-verified end to end; existing test suite plus new coverage for the locking rules all pass.
close_reason: Shipped: /panel/codes split into list/new/detail pages. CRUD-safety enforced with never-delete: type+scopes lock on AccessCode::scopeAndTypeLocked() (uses_count>0), royalty terms lock on RoyaltyTerm::hasBeenCharged() (appeared in any usage_event.fees_due). New GET /codes/{code} + PATCH /terms/{term} endpoints. Route binding switched to opaque code string. 50 tests pass incl. 3 new; full flow browser-verified incl. lock engagement after simulated usage, mobile responsive.

title: DECISION: panel permission model for norm promotion
description: Norm-set promotion changes live scoring output and currently requires only the general 'admin' role — same bar as rotating an API key. Decide: move to named permissions (e.g. keys.manage, codes.manage, norms.promote) or add a narrower third role (e.g. norms-reviewer) so promotion access can be granted separately from general admin. All panel users should keep read access to norms/impact reports regardless.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded on permission model shape (named permissions vs additional role); unblocks prolog-ipd implementation.
close_reason: Decided: add a third panel role 'norms-reviewer' alongside admin/viewer, rather than a full named-permissions system. Minimal change to the existing single-role-column design. Unblocks prolog-ipd.

title: DECISION: clients table model — single entity vs clients/payees split
description: Three free-text fields currently stand in for 'who is this for' with no shared entity: api_keys.name, access_codes.issued_to, royalty_terms.recipient. First two are naturally the same thing (the paying client). royalty_terms.recipient is often a third party being PAID OUT (content owner, translator, partner) — not necessarily the same client entity. Decide: one normalized clients table used for all three, or a separate payees/recipients table for royalty_terms.recipient distinct from the paying clients table? This gates prolog-opw.1's schema and blocks prolog-opw.2 (Stripe billing needs a stable client entity to attach a stripe_customer_id to).
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded on single-entity vs clients+payees split; unblocks prolog-opw.1 schema design.
close_reason: Decided: separate clients (who pays) and payees (who gets paid via royalty_terms) tables — not one shared entity. A payee MAY optionally link to a client record when the same real-world entity is both a paying client and a royalty recipient, but that link is nullable/optional since many payees (translators, content owners) never have a client record. Unblocks prolog-opw.1.

title: DECISION: retake/same-person identity and variance tracking
description: User question: can we detect the same person retaking the assessment, reuse their prior session data, and track variance over time? Needs answers before anything is buildable: (1) how to detect 'same person' (email? external_id? caller-supplied order id? fuzzy matching in scope?); (2) what 'reuse session' means (skip re-answering unchanged tools, vs something session/auth-related); (3) what 'track variance' means as a deliverable (new panel timeline view? diff between scored_results? export?) and who consumes it; (4) whether 'person' and 'order' are the same identity concept for this purpose vs for prolog-4iu.4's pay-once-per-order royalty logic.
acceptance_criteria: Answers recorded for all four questions; unblocks prolog-4iu.4 and prolog-4iu.5 implementation.
close_reason: Decided, three parts: (1) same-person detection uses caller-supplied external_id as primary signal (scoped per api_key), falling back to exact email match when external_id is absent — no fuzzy matching. (2) 'Reuse session' means no reuse: each retake is a fully independent submission, only linked to prior assessments for the same person for reporting/identity purposes. (3) 'Track variance' means a new panel timeline view: pick a person, see all their linked assessments/scored_results across time with score deltas between takes. Unblocks prolog-4iu.4 and prolog-4iu.5.

title: DECISION: access-code human-readable naming model
description: Codes are opaque (ac_xxxx) by design for security (never a brand name). User wants royalty statements reportable by code/product, which needs a name. Decide: is 'name' a free field set per-code at issue time, or derived from product_code + client (since one product can be issued under multiple codes to different partners with different names)?
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded on where the display name comes from (per-code field vs derived), ready to implement on access_codes.
close_reason: Decided: add a free-text 'name' field on access_codes, set at issue time (e.g. 'Acme Corp - Q3 training batch'). Not derived from product+client — supports one product issued under multiple differently-named codes. Unblocks prolog-4iu.2.

title: DECISION: access-code type as label vs royalty_terms-driven policy
description: Currently access_codes.type (training|bizdev|derivative) is treated as what determines royalty policy (derivative = no royalty due by convention). Decide: should type become a purely descriptive/internal label, with royalty behavior driven entirely by whether the code has active royalty_terms rows (a code with zero active terms simply owes nothing, regardless of type)? Or should type be dropped/replaced entirely? Blocks prolog-4iu.1 implementation and the panel Codes.tsx UI (which currently groups/tones by type).
acceptance_criteria: A clear answer recorded on whether type stays as a label-only field or is removed, and how royalty-due-or-not is determined going forward.
close_reason: Decided: type becomes a display-only/internal label. Royalty-due-or-not is driven entirely by whether the code has active royalty_terms rows — a code with zero active terms owes nothing regardless of type. Unblocks prolog-4iu.1.

title: Codes & Royalties panel: expose existing update/add-term/end-term endpoints in the UI (no edit/revoke/manage-terms controls today)
description:
User observation while reviewing /panel/codes: there's no way to manage (edit/revoke) codes or their royalty terms in the UI — confirmed correct. This is a UI completeness gap, not a missing backend feature: the endpoints already exist and are just never called from panel/src/views/Codes.tsx.

## What exists server-side (api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/CodesController.php, routed in api/routes/panel.php under panel.role:admin)
- `PATCH /codes/{code}` (CodesController::update) — can change `active`, `max_uses`, `expires_at`, `issued_to`, `notes`. **Never called from the UI.**
- `POST /codes/{code}/terms` (CodesController::addTerm) — adds a new royalty term (recipient, kind, amount, currency, effective_from/until). **Never called from the UI.**
- `POST /terms/{term}/end` (CodesController::endTerm) — soft-ends a term (`active=false`, `effective_until=now()`) — the docs/07-mandated 'end, never delete' pattern. **Never called from the UI.**

## What the UI actually has (panel/src/views/Codes.tsx)
- An 'Issue codes' form — create only (calls `POST /codes`).
- A read-only 'Codes' table — shows `code`, `type`, `allowed_scopes`, `issued_to`, uses, and royalty terms flattened as plain text (`t.recipient: t.amount t.currency${t.active ? '' : ' (ended)'}`) with NO buttons to add a term, end a term, edit the code's fields, or revoke/deactivate a code.
- Compare to the sibling Keys view (panel/src/views/Keys.tsx), which DOES have this pattern already: a working 'Revoke/Restore' toggle (`PATCH /keys/{id}` with `active`) — so there's an established UI convention in this codebase to follow.

Note: true hard-delete is intentionally absent everywhere here (docs/07: 'manage a code's royalty terms — add/end without deleting history') — so 'full CRUD' in this domain correctly means Create/Read/Update/Deactivate-or-End, not literal delete. That part of the design is fine as-is.

## Scope for the fix (not implemented here)
- Add per-code edit affordance in the Codes table/row: toggle active (revoke/restore, mirroring Keys.tsx), and edit max_uses/expires_at/issued_to/notes (e.g. inline edit or a small edit form/modal).
- Add an 'Add royalty term' action per code (small form: recipient, kind, amount, currency, effective_from/until) calling `POST /codes/{code}/terms`.
- Add an 'End term' button per active royalty term calling `POST /terms/{term}/end`.
- Gate all of the above on `isAdmin` (existing convention in this file) — and note this will intersect with prolog-4iu.1's planned rework of `type`-driven royalty policy and prolog-ipd's granular-permissions work (norms promotion vs. general admin) — worth checking those don't also want a narrower permission than plain 'admin' for editing royalty terms specifically, since terms changes affect money owed.

Depends on prolog-4iu.1 (type-vs-royalty-terms policy rework) being decided first, so this UI isn't built against a code/royalty-term shape that's about to change.
close_reason: Manage panel on /panel/codes: revoke/restore code, add royalty term (all kinds incl. on-conversion, optional language), end term. All existing endpoints now reachable from the UI; verified in-browser end to end.

title: Stripe-based monthly usage-metered billing with client self-service portal (bank transfer collection)
description:
User: 'I think that we will use stripe to collect the royalties from the client by bank transfer on a monthly usage meter — the client would manage their billing in their account.'

Breaking down the ask (research/scoping only, not implemented):
1. **Usage-metered billing**: roll up `usage_events`/`fees_due` per client per month into a Stripe metered-usage subscription/invoice item. platform/docs/07-access-codes-royalties.md already anticipates this exact shape: 'When Stripe is enabled: map billable clients → Stripe customers, roll up non-derivative fees_due line items into metered usage / invoices (Laravel Cashier)... Royalty statements... must be producible from usage_events alone — no Stripe dependency for reporting.' So the metering source of truth (`usage_events.fees_due`, api/app/Models/UsageEvent.php) already exists and is designed to not depend on Stripe for reporting — this issue is about the collection side.
2. **Collection via bank transfer**: Stripe supports ACH/bank-transfer-funded invoices (e.g. Stripe Invoicing with 'customer_balance'/bank transfer payment method, or ACH Direct Debit) rather than card payment — needs explicit configuration decision on which Stripe payment method/flow to use for this (ACH direct debit vs. bank-transfer-to-pay-invoice vs. wire).
3. **Client self-service billing portal**: 'the client would manage their billing in their account' — implies either (a) Stripe's own hosted Billing Portal (stripe-php `BillingPortal::create()`) linked from somewhere the client can reach, or (b) a custom-built client-facing account area. Currently there is NO client-facing surface at all — panel/ (docs/08) is explicitly the internal admin/ops panel; there is no client login, account, or portal today. This likely means either standing up a new client-facing app/area, or embedding Stripe's hosted billing portal behind some client auth we don't yet have.
4. Depends on prolog-opw.1 (normalized `clients` table) for a stable entity to attach `stripe_customer_id` (and possibly `stripe_subscription_id`/payment-method-on-file state) to.

Open questions to resolve before implementation (flagged, not answered here):
- Does 'the client's account' mean a brand-new authenticated client portal (separate from the internal panel), or just handing them a Stripe-hosted billing portal link via email/dashboard?
- Cadence/timing: bill on a fixed monthly calendar date, or per-client billing anniversary?
- Which Stripe product: Billing (subscriptions + metered usage) + Invoicing with bank transfer, vs. a simpler manual monthly invoice per client generated from `usage_events` and pushed to Stripe.
- Currency/multi-currency handling — `royalty_terms.currency` already varies per term (api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php) — need to confirm Stripe billing can be per-client single-currency or must support mixed currencies per client.
- No Cashier/Stripe package appears installed yet in the API (api/composer.json) — confirm during implementation planning.
notes: STALE-CONTEXT NOTE (2026-07-13): metering source is now the charges + payouts ledgers (docs/07), NOT usage_events.fees_due. Payouts carry status accrued|paid|void; clients.stripe_customer_id exists (opw.1 done). Monthly rollup = charges per client per period; Stripe here collects charges IN from clients (payouts OUT to payees are separate, see prolog-9x0). Still needs: Stripe account/keys, ACH vs bank-transfer vs wire choice, hosted Billing Portal vs custom client area, per-client currency policy. Practically also wants a deployed environment first (prolog-3cj) — noted here since bd can't dep a task on an epic.

title: Add a normalized clients table; replace free-text api_keys.name / access_codes.issued_to / royalty_terms.recipient
description:
Currently there are (at least) THREE independent free-text fields that each informally represent 'who is this for', with no shared entity or FK between them:
- `api_keys.name` — labeled 'Client name' in the panel UI (panel/src/views/Keys.tsx) but stored as a plain string on ApiKey (api/app/Models/ApiKey.php).
- `access_codes.issued_to` — nullable free-text string on AccessCode (api/app/Models/AccessCode.php), shown as 'Issued to' in panel/src/views/Codes.tsx.
- `royalty_terms.recipient` — free-text string identifying who is owed a royalty (api/app/Models/RoyaltyTerm.php), shown inline in the Codes table and royalty statement (panel/src/views/Codes.tsx: `t.recipient`, and `statement.totals_by_recipient` keyed by this string).

None of these are validated against each other — e.g. nothing stops 'Acme Corp' vs 'ACME Corp.' being used inconsistently across an API key's name, a code's issued_to, and a royalty term's recipient for what should be the same client, which would silently fragment royalty statements and billing.

Ask: introduce a normalized `clients` table (name, billing contact/email, notes, active flag, and later a Stripe customer id — see sibling issue prolog-opw.2) and:
- Add `client_id` FK to `api_keys` (replacing or supplementing the free-text `name` — decide whether a key keeps its own display name or always inherits the client's name).
- Add `client_id` FK to `access_codes` (replacing `issued_to`).
- Decide whether `royalty_terms.recipient` should also become a `client_id` FK (recipients are often third parties being paid OUT, e.g. content owners/translators/partners — not necessarily the same 'client' entity as the paying customer, so this may need a distinct `payees`/`recipients` table rather than reusing `clients`; needs a decision, not assumed here).
- Update panel/src/views/Keys.tsx and panel/src/views/Codes.tsx forms to select an existing client (dropdown/typeahead) instead of free-typing a name, plus a way to manage clients themselves (new panel view, e.g. /panel/clients).
- Update KeysController and CodesController (api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/*.php) validation/store logic accordingly.

This is a prerequisite for prolog-opw.2 (Stripe billing), since Stripe customer mapping needs a stable client entity to attach a `stripe_customer_id` to.
close_reason: Shipped: clients + payees tables with backfill migration, FKs replacing api_keys.name-as-client/access_codes.issued_to/payout_terms.recipient (payouts ledger keeps name snapshots), panel CRUD (deactivate-never-delete), client selects on keys/codes, payee select + quick-add on payout lines. 50 tests pass; browser-verified.

title: Panel: tables collapse to stacked cards on mobile
description: User feedback: horizontal-scroll tables are a bad idea on mobile — every panel table should degrade to stacked cards below the sm breakpoint. Introduce a data-driven DataTable component (columns + rows instead of raw <tr> children) rendering a classic table on sm+ and label/value cards on mobile, with a designated primary column as card title and row actions in the card footer. Convert Keys, Codes, Assessments, Norms, Pipeline dependency-matrix tables.
close_reason: DataTable ships: table on sm+, stacked cards on mobile with primary-column titles and footer actions. All five panel tables converted; old Table removed. Verified at 375px.

title: Open question: retake/rescoring — reuse prior session data, detect same person, track variance over time
description:
User, thinking out loud while discussing royalty-on-conversion (see sibling prolog-4iu.4): 'Can we rescore an order for a particular user — if we know someone took it once and they are taking it again can we reuse their session? Will it track their retake and variance over time?' This is flagged explicitly as an open question needing a decision, not a ready-to-build spec — filed here so it isn't lost, per user's request to just capture issues for later.

Current state (from codebase research):
- Each `assessments` row (api/app/Models/Assessment.php) is a fully independent intake — firstname/lastname/email/language/gender/dob plus its own `assessment_tools` (one row per tool, replaced on resubmit within the SAME assessment) and one-or-more `scored_results` (one per scoring call against that assessment). There is no linkage between two separate `assessments` rows for what is actually the same human retaking the test later — a retake today just creates a brand-new `assessments` row with no back-reference.
- `external_id` (nullable, indexed with `api_key_id`) is the only existing field that could correlate submissions from the same caller/system, but it's optional, not unique/enforced, and not currently used to detect or link retakes.
- No 'variance over time' or retake-comparison feature exists anywhere in the codebase (checked panel/src/views/Assessments.tsx and api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/AssessmentsController.php — assessment show/audit views show a single assessment's results/audit trail, not a timeline across multiple assessments for the same person).
- `scored_results.norm_set` is recorded per result (docs/06 reproducibility contract: 'nothing ever silently rescores') — any retake/variance feature must respect that each historical result stays pinned to the norm set it was scored under, even if norms change between the original take and the retake.

Questions to resolve before this becomes buildable (captured from user's message, not answered here):
1. How do we detect 'this is the same person taking it again' — by email? by `external_id`? by a caller-supplied person/order identifier? Is fuzzy-matching (same email, different name spelling) in scope?
2. 'Reuse their session' — does this mean reusing previously-submitted tool responses (skip re-answering) if a retake comes in with the same/overlapping tools, or does it mean something session/auth related?
3. 'Track their retake and variance over time' — is this a new panel view (timeline of a person's results across multiple assessments), a computed diff between two `scored_results`, or a data export? Who consumes it (internal ops, the client/API caller)?
4. How does this interact with the royalty-due-once-per-order logic in prolog-4iu.4 — is 'order' the same identity concept as 'person for retake purposes', or a separate one (e.g. one person could have multiple distinct paid orders over time, each billable, but retakes within a single order should not double-bill)?

Depends on: a decision here likely blocks/informs prolog-4iu.4 (pay-on-conversion royalty), since 'has this order already been charged' requires the same identity/correlation mechanism as 'is this the same person retaking'.
close_reason: Person linkage implemented per decision (samePersonQuery: external_id primary, email fallback, no fuzzy). Panel person-timeline endpoint + UI card with rank deltas between consecutive takes, verified in-browser. No answer reuse — takes stay independent.

title: New royalty kind: 'due on conversion' for free lead-gen assessments, charged exactly once per order/person
description:
User's train of thought (captured verbatim intent): 'We do need a type of royalty that is only due when the product is paid for — so sometimes we provide free assessments as an advertising lead. If we capture and convert those then it has to be paid for. So... I think this would be a subset of the tools in a code with no royalty due. Then if that same order is scored again or comes in with more data and uses a different code the royalty for that code would be applied... however the royalty is only paid once — as soon as it is collected for a particular order it is not paid again.'

Interpretation / scenario:
1. A free/lead-gen assessment is scored under a code with a reduced tool/scope subset and no royalty due (this part maps cleanly onto the existing `derivative`-style 'no royalty' code, or a new explicit 'lead-gen' code type — ties into prolog-4iu.1's type-vs-royalty-terms rework).
2. If that lead later converts (buys), the same person/order is rescored — likely under a different, paying access code with more scopes/tools — and a royalty becomes due at that point.
3. Critically: the royalty must be charged exactly once per underlying order/person, even though there are now 2+ `usage_events`/`scored_results` rows (the free one and the paid one). Nothing currently tracks 'has a royalty already been collected for this order' across multiple scoring events for what is conceptually the same respondent/order.

Current gaps found in the codebase:
- `usage_events.fees_due` (api/app/Models/UsageEvent.php) is computed independently per event via `AccessCode::feesDueNow()` (api/app/Models/AccessCode.php) — there is no concept of an 'order' that spans multiple assessments/usage events, and no dedup/once-only-charge logic.
- `assessments.external_id` (nullable, api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000002_create_assessment_tables.php, indexed with api_key_id) is the closest existing hook for correlating multiple assessment submissions to the same external order/person, but it's optional and not currently used for any royalty-dedup purpose — confirm with caller whether `external_id` is meant to be this correlation key, or whether a new explicit 'order' entity is needed.
- No royalty_terms `kind` for 'due on conversion' exists (current kinds: flat_per_report | percentage_of_price | tiered | subscription — migration 2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php).

This needs real product design before implementation (not just an engineering estimate) — see sibling issue prolog-4iu.5 which captures the related open question about whether/how to detect and reuse a prior session for the same person on retake, which this conversion-royalty logic would depend on for correctly identifying 'same order'.
close_reason: flat_on_conversion kind: charged once per person (external_id per key, email fallback), dedup via prior usage_events fee rows by term id. Tested: same person never charged twice, different person charged.

title: Royalty terms: support language-scoped royalties (e.g. translator fees)
description:
User: 'We sometimes pay a royalty on language to a translator so language should be included.'

Current state:
- `royalty_terms` (api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php) has no `language` column — terms apply to a code regardless of the language the assessment/scoring was performed in.
- `assessments.language` and `scored_results.language` already exist (migration 2026_07_11_000002_create_assessment_tables.php) and are recorded per scoring event, so the data needed to evaluate a language-scoped term is already present at usage-event time.
- `AccessCode::feesDueNow()` (api/app/Models/AccessCode.php) evaluates all active royalty terms for a code with no language filter; `UsageEvent.fees_due` (api/app/Models/UsageEvent.php) records whatever `feesDueNow()` returns — would need to become language-aware, presumably by passing the event's language into `feesDueNow()` and filtering/matching terms that specify a language (nullable = applies to all languages).

Ask: extend `royalty_terms` with an optional `language` (nullable — nullable means 'applies to all languages') so a translator/localization royalty can be configured per-language on a code (e.g. 'pay $X per report to the FR translator, only for fr-language reports'), and have fee computation at scoring time filter terms by the assessment's language.

Depends on / should be sequenced with prolog-4iu's sibling issue about moving away from type-driven royalty policy, since both touch `royalty_terms` schema and `AccessCode::feesDueNow()`.
close_reason: royalty_terms.language (nullable=all); feesDueNow filters by event language; addTerm + panel support language selection. Tested: fr translator fee fires on fr only.

title: Codes: add a human-readable name field so royalty statements can report by product/code
description:
User: 'The royalty statement should be able to report on codes (therefore products) so codes should have names.'

Current state:
- `access_codes` table (api/database/migrations/2026_07_11_000001_create_access_control_tables.php) has no `name` column — only the opaque `code` string (e.g. `ac_xxxx`, intentionally unguessable/never a brand name per AccessCode::generateCode() docblock), `product_code` (a ProductCatalog reference, see docs/04), and `issued_to` (client/org).
- CodesController::statement() (api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/CodesController.php) currently groups royalty totals by `recipient` only (`totals_by_recipient`), not by code or product — see the `Statement` type in panel/src/views/Codes.tsx (`totals_by_recipient: Record<string, Record<string, number>>`), no per-code/product breakdown.
- panel/src/views/Codes.tsx's Codes table shows the raw `code` string, not a name.

Ask: add a `name` (or similar) field to `access_codes` for display/reporting purposes — distinct from the opaque `code` value which must remain unguessable/non-brand-identifying for security reasons (docs/07 explicitly: 'A code is never itself a product's brand name'). The name would be an internal-only label (not exposed to the scoring API), used so:
- The Issue-codes form (panel/src/views/Codes.tsx) can capture a name at issue time.
- The Codes table and royalty statement can be sliced/reported by code name (and thus by product), not just by recipient.

Needs a decision on whether 'name' is per-code (each issued code has its own name) or should instead be derived from `product_code` (ProductCatalog) + `issued_to`, since a product can be issued under multiple codes with different names/partners (docs/07: 'A single product can also be issued under more than one code').
close_reason: access_codes.name added (required at issue), statement reports totals_by_code, CSV has code_name column, panel form + table show names. Tested.

title: Codes: move away from type-driven royalty policy toward per-code revenue/split configuration
description:
Currently `access_codes.type` (training|bizdev|derivative, immutable enum) is treated as the thing that determines royalty policy (platform/docs/07 table: training/bizdev 'per royalty terms', derivative 'no royalty due'). The user's framing: type is really just a *label/description* of a set of royalty agreements, not the mechanism itself — the actual mechanism should be 'specify the total royalty payment expected (revenue) when a scoring is performed and how it is divided out to different parties', which is close to what `royalty_terms` already models (recipient, kind, amount, currency — many per code) but isn't surfaced or framed that way in the panel UI (panel/src/views/Codes.tsx groups by `type` badge, not by 'is there a revenue split configured').

Needs product/design decision (flagged, not answered here):
- Should `type` (training/bizdev/derivative) be kept purely as a free-text/label field (for internal categorization) while royalty behavior is driven entirely by whether `royalty_terms` rows exist (i.e. 'derivative' stops being special-cased for 'no royalty' and instead a code with zero active royalty_terms simply owes nothing, regardless of type)?
- Or should type be dropped/replaced with something else entirely?
- CodesController (api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/CodesController.php) and platform/docs/07-access-codes-royalties.md's 'code type (business-critical distinction)' framing would need to change to match whatever is decided.
- panel/src/views/Codes.tsx's Issue-codes form and the royalty statement UI currently key off `type` for tone/grouping — would need rework once the policy shifts to being purely `royalty_terms`-driven.

This depends on/relates to prolog-27y and prolog-5q8 (both touch the Codes.tsx form) — worth sequencing after this decision is made so those UI issues aren't built against a model that's about to change.
close_reason: Type is now label-only; royalty behavior fully terms-driven. Statement + CSV + panel reworked; derivative special-casing removed everywhere. Tested.

title: Panel: introduce granular permission management — restrict norm review/promotion separately from general admin
description:
User request: add user permission management for the panel. Everyone with panel access should be able to *see* norm review/impact/promotion info, but the actual review-and-promote actions (and anything that changes scoring calculations) must NOT be clickable/actionable except for a specifically permitted subset of users — this should be a distinct permission from general admin capabilities.

Current state (binary role model):
- panel/src/auth.tsx: `User.role` is typed as `'admin' | 'viewer'` only.
- api/app/Http/Middleware/EnsurePanelRole.php: single check `$role === 'admin' && $user->role !== 'admin'` — no concept of finer-grained permissions, just admin vs not-admin. Comment even says 'Panel roles (docs/08): viewer sees everything, admin changes things.'
- platform/docs/08-control-panel.md line 3: 'Roles: admin, viewer (extendable)' — explicitly flagged as a future extension point.
- api/routes/panel.php: ALL mutating endpoints (keys create/update/rotate, codes issue/update/terms, AND norms impact/promote/retire) are gated behind the same `panel.role:admin` middleware group (lines 41-54). There is no separation between 'can manage API keys/codes/billing' and 'can promote/retire norm sets (i.e. change scoring calculations)'.
- panel/src/views/Norms.tsx: frontend also just checks `isAdmin` (user?.role === 'admin') to show the 'Run impact' / 'Promote' / 'Retire' buttons (lines 34, 97, 107) — same single flag.
- Norms promotion is high-stakes: per platform/docs/06-norms.md and the Norms.tsx Explainer text, promoting a norm set changes the raw→normed conversion table used going forward, i.e. it changes scoring outputs. This should have a narrower blast radius of who can trigger it than 'anyone who can rotate an API key or issue a code'.

Requested change (design + implement later, not in this issue):
- Move from a binary admin/viewer role to a proper permission system (e.g. named permissions like `keys.manage`, `codes.manage`, `norms.promote`, or a small set of roles like admin / norms-reviewer / viewer) so that:
  - All authenticated panel users can continue to view norm sets, impact reports, and sample accumulation (read-only, as today).
  - Only users explicitly granted a norms-promotion permission can call POST /norms/{slug}/impact, /promote, /retire — separately from whoever can manage keys/codes.
  - Frontend buttons in Norms.tsx (and any other 'changes calculations' action) are gated on the specific permission, not just `role === 'admin'`.
- Needs: a users/roles/permissions data model (currently just a `role` string column presumably on the panel user model — verify), new middleware/gate (extending or replacing EnsurePanelRole), updated route gating in panel.php, and updated frontend checks in auth.tsx/Norms.tsx.
- Should also double check whether any other admin-only actions besides norms promote/retire are similarly 'changes calculations' and deserve the same restricted permission (e.g. content/pipeline mutations if any exist later).
notes: DECISION MADE (prolog-4ix, 2026-07-12): add a third role 'norms-reviewer' alongside admin/viewer — NOT a full permissions system. Implementation shape: users.role gains the value; EnsurePanelRole/panel.php gate norms impact/promote/retire on admin OR norms-reviewer (or norms-reviewer only — confirm whether general admins should ALSO promote, or be excluded so only reviewers can); Norms.tsx button gating + auth.tsx type; panel:user --role accepts it. docs/08 role line update.

title: Panel: proper-case labels in Content/Codes selector dropdowns (not data-analysis views like Pipeline)
description:
User feedback (clarified): scope is narrower than initially filed. The user does NOT want raw identifiers changed/relabeled in data-analysis/result views (e.g. 127.0.0.1:8000/panel/pipeline, where scope codes and tool names appear as part of the dataset being inspected — leave those as-is, no modification needed there).

The proper-case treatment should apply specifically to the *selector* controls (dropdowns/inputs used to pick a value while performing an action) on:
- 127.0.0.1:8000/panel/content — the tool `<select>` in panel/src/views/Content.tsx (`TOOLS` array: `reflections`, `personalmotivators`, `areamissions`, `abilitiesfilter`, `personalstyle`, `personalexpectations`, `person`, `role`, `organization` — currently rendered as raw lowercase option text, e.g. 'areamissions' instead of 'Area Missions').
- 127.0.0.1:8000/panel/codes — the 'Type' `<select>` in the 'Issue codes' form in panel/src/views/Codes.tsx (`<option value=\"training\">training</option>`, `bizdev`, `derivative` — option display text should be proper case, e.g. 'Training', 'Bizdev'/'Biz Dev', 'Derivative'). The 'Allowed scopes' input on this same page is also a selector-type control per prolog-5q8's proposed redesign, so once that becomes a dropdown/multiselect it should also use proper-case labels for scope names.

Explicitly OUT of scope for this issue: table/badge displays of result/analysis data such as panel/src/views/Pipeline.tsx's scope column, panel/src/views/Norms.tsx status badges, panel/src/views/Assessments.tsx norm_set badge, etc. — those are data being reviewed, not selectors, and the user does not want them modified.

This is UI-display-only for the selector option labels — the underlying `value` sent to the API (e.g. `option value=\"training\"`, tool query param `areamissions`) must NOT change, since scoring/content/DB logic depends on the exact lowercase identifiers.

Suggested approach (design/implement later, not in this issue):
- Add a small shared label-mapping helper/dictionary (e.g. `panel/src/labels.ts`) mapping known raw identifiers (code types, tool names, scope codes) to proper-case display strings, used only for the option/label text in these two selectors (value attributes stay unchanged).
- Coordinate with prolog-5q8 (scopes multiselect/explainer on /panel/codes) so the scope name mapping is built once and reused there.
close_reason: Selector labels proper-cased via shared panel/src/labels.ts (Content tool dropdown, Codes type dropdown); values unchanged; data-analysis views untouched per clarified scope. SCOPE_LABELS pre-built for prolog-5q8. Browser-verified.

title: Panel UI: redesign as mobile-first modern control panel (Tailwind + Lucide-style)
description:
The admin panel (panel/) works but its UI is basic/desktop-only and doesn't match the modern 'control panel' aesthetic the user wants (references tailwind/lucide.dev-style design: clean cards, icon-driven nav, generous spacing, dark-mode-capable, responsive).

Current state:
- panel/src/App.tsx renders a single fixed top header (`max-w-6xl` container) with a horizontal `<nav>` of text-only links (NAV const) — no hamburger/drawer for small screens, so nav links will wrap/overflow on mobile widths instead of collapsing.
- panel/src/components/ui.tsx is a small shared component kit (Card, Badge, Table, Field, Button, Explainer, Bars) — plain Tailwind utility classes, no icon library, no dark mode tokens, no responsive variants beyond default block layout. Buttons/badges/cards are functional but visually minimal (flat colors, no elevation/gradient/micro-interaction polish).
- Tables (e.g. Codes.tsx, Keys.tsx, Assessments.tsx etc.) are plain `<table>` markup wrapped in `overflow-x-auto` — usable but not mobile-friendly (no card/list fallback at small breakpoints).
- Stack: React 19 + react-router-dom 7 + Tailwind CSS v4 (via @tailwindcss/vite) + Vite. No component/icon library installed yet (no lucide-react, no headlessui/radix).
- Views affected: Dashboard, Keys, Codes, Assessments, Norms, Pipeline, Content, Login (all under panel/src/views/).

Requested direction (tracked here for later design/implementation, NOT implemented in this issue):
- Mobile-first layout: collapsible/drawer nav (hamburger on small screens, sidebar or top bar on larger), touch-friendly spacing/tap targets, tables that degrade to stacked cards on narrow viewports.
- Modern control-panel visual language similar to Tailwind UI / lucide.dev sites: icon-driven nav (would need to add an icon library such as lucide-react), softer shadows/elevation, refined typography scale, possibly dark mode support.
- Likely needs a broader design pass across panel/src/components/ui.tsx (the shared kit) plus per-view layout updates (App.tsx shell/nav, and each view in panel/src/views/) rather than a single isolated fix.

Suggested next step: scope this into a design plan/mockup (e.g. Plan mode) before implementation, since it touches shell layout + shared UI kit + every view.
close_reason: Mobile-first redesign shipped: lucide-react icon sidebar (desktop) + hamburger/slide-over drawer (mobile), refreshed kit (rounded-xl cards, focus rings, StatCard icons, touch targets, wrapping forms, momentum-scroll tables). Verified at desktop + 375px. Deferred: dark mode and table->stacked-card fallback (kept horizontal scroll) — refile if wanted.

title: Panel: replace 'Allowed scopes' free-text input with an informed multiselect on Codes issue form
description:
On /panel/codes, the 'Issue codes' form (Card 'Issue codes' in panel/src/views/Codes.tsx) has an 'Allowed scopes' field that is a raw comma-separated text input (form.scopes, default 'full'), split on ',' client-side and sent as allowed_scopes to POST /codes. The API validates scopes server-side via App\\Scoring\\Scopes::expand() and rejects unknown scopes with a 422, but the UI gives no guidance on valid values, so admins must already know the exact scope strings.

Valid scopes (api/app/Scoring/Scopes.php SCOPES const) are: mcs.m, mcs.c, mcs.s, mcs, pro.person, pro.role, pro.org, insights, reflections — plus the alias 'full' which expands to all of them. Each scope maps to specific tools/data it exposes (see Scopes::SCOPES and platform/docs/04 dependency matrix) and some are gendered (touch S/P norm dimensions).

Proposed follow-up (do not implement yet, just tracked here): replace the free-text input with either (a) a multiselect dropdown listing the known scopes (plus 'full'), or (b) a drawer/page that lists each scope with a plain-language explanation of what data/tools it grants access to, so the person issuing a code can make an informed choice instead of guessing/copy-pasting scope strings. Should also handle 'full' as an explicit option (or a toggle) rather than a magic string.

Relevant files:
- panel/src/views/Codes.tsx (lines ~34, 44-53, 108-110 — the form state and 'Allowed scopes (comma-sep)' Field)
- api/app/Scoring/Scopes.php (SCOPES const, expand())
- api/app/Http/Controllers/Panel/CodesController.php (validation of allowed_scopes)
- platform/docs/07-access-codes-royalties.md (scope/allowed-scopes model docs)
close_reason: Chip multiselect from shared SCOPE_LABELS with plain-language names + raw codes; 'full' exclusive toggle; API values unchanged. E2E-verified: issued code stored exactly the selected scopes.
